mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window or positive returns dominate during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output, which translates to positive short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding the 2.5% annualized threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >70% of months where inflation breaches the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between weak nonfarm payrolls growth and rising unemployment signals a structural labor market deterioration, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of weak payrolls and rising unemployment predicts positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate economic activity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive short-term market returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances.
